statement.p: A graph of a function f is given. Use the graph to write a formula for f in vertex form. You will need to identify the vertex and also one more point on the graph to find the leading coefficient a.

solution.p: A parabolas vertex form looks like f(x) = a(x-h)^2+k.
We can see the vertex is at (-1,-3), we have f(x) = a{\left(x+1\right)^{2}-3}.

Next, we can see the parabola passes the point (1,{1}). (We could choose to use another point on the parabola.) We substitute this point (1,{1}) into f(x) = a{\left(x+1\right)^{2}-3}, and solve for a:

p.m: \displaystyle{\begin{aligned}[t] f(x) \amp = a{\left(x+1\right)^{2}-3} \\ {1} \amp = a(1 + 1)^2 - 3 \\ {1} \amp = a(2)^2 - 3 \\ {1} \amp = 4 a - 3 \\ {4} \amp = 4 a \\ a \amp = 1 \end{aligned} }
